newsSinceJun6: No new company-specific fundamentals since June 6: no earnings (next report July 30), no contract or guidance news from AXT itself. The -13.6% June 9 flush (now ~35% off the early-June ~$120 high) is a de-rating/flow event: CEO Morris Young's Form 4 (197,498 shares sold June 1-2 at $107.70-$121.48, ~$22M, disclosed last week) deepened distribution fears after a +368% YTD parabolic run, amplified by circulating bear narratives - one industry stakeholder rejecting the InP-bottleneck thesis and a competitor's 7-year supply pact with Sumitomo Electric. Offsetting: Northland raised its PT to $125 from $90 on June 5, backlog >$100M, and the staged InP capacity doubling through 2027 (funded by the April $632.5M raise) remains on track. Judgment: today's move is predominantly flow-driven (insider-sale overhang + crowding unwind), with a minor fundamental-narrative component (bottleneck skepticism); the core 40%-share InP substrate thesis is questioned but not broken.

sources.label: StockTitan - AXT CEO exercises options, sells 197,498 shares (Form 4)
TipRanks - AXT Stock Tumbles as Insiders Cash In
Yahoo Finance - Why AXT Is Down 7.8% After $632.5M InP Expansion Equity Raise
Seeking Alpha - AXT plans to double InP capacity again in 2027, Q2 GAAP EPS guide $0.05-$0.07
GuruFocus - AXTI Faces Caution Due to InP Supply Chain Concerns

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already cut the name for post-rerating crowding, and today's -13.6% flush (~35% off the high) is exactly that de-rating playing out - it partially restores asymmetry on this crowding-sensitive alpha screen, so no second down-move. But the flush was triggered by CEO distribution and InP-bottleneck skepticism, not pure flow, so it doesn't earn an upgrade either.
verdicts.bull: AXT holds ~40% of the global InP substrate market feeding optical interconnects/CPO, carries a >$100M AI-tied backlog, and a fully funded capacity doubling through 2027; a 35% drawdown with the bottleneck position intact rebuilds asymmetric upside, and Northland just raised its PT to $125.
verdicts.neutral: Still +368% YTD at a $7.2B cap on roughly $100M annualized revenue; one washout day does not make the name hidden again, and the next hard datapoint is July 30 earnings, leaving the stock hostage to narrative flow until then.
verdicts.bear: The CEO sold ~$22M at the top right after a dilutive $632.5M raise, an industry stakeholder publicly rejected the InP-bottleneck premise, and a 7-year supply agreement went to competitor Sumitomo - if the bottleneck narrative cracks, an equity-financed capacity doubling becomes a margin and dilution trap.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 earnings/guidance on July 30 confirming InP backlog conversion and 6-inch InP qualification with AI optical-interconnect customers.
verdicts.primaryRisk: The InP-bottleneck thesis deflates (customers dual-source via Sumitomo or design around InP) just as equity-funded capacity comes online, compounded by continued insider distribution.
